steps.title: Set up distance and time
steps.content: The total distance is $$r+b=240$$ Since time equals distance divided by speed, the biking time is $\frac{b}{12}$ and the running time is $\frac{r}{6}$.

steps.title: Use the time relationship
steps.content: Riley biked for twice as many hours as she ran, so $$\frac{b}{12}=2\left(\frac{r}{6}\right)$$ Multiplying by $12$ gives $$b=4r$$

steps.title: Solve and confirm
steps.content: Substitute $b=4r$ into the distance equation: $$r+4r=240$$ $$r=48$$ Then $$b=4(48)=192$$ Choice D (‘192’) is correct.
